meta.designer_notes: A gritty dark fantasy roster where heroes are expendable but meaningful. Five combat classes cover tank (Shieldbearer), damage (Shadow Hunter), support/loot (Warden), bruiser (Plague Veteran), and speed (Outrunner). Three artisan classes staff the Forge, Shadow Vault, and Guildhall.

nodes.label: Shieldbearer
nodes.description: Frontline guardian — highest DEF and HP on the roster with a survival passive that hardens when wounded, making them nearly unkillable but slow to clear expeditions.

unique_passive.description: DEF ×1.8 when HP drops below 30% — the shield refuses to fall.

nodes.label: Shadow Hunter
nodes.description: Swift assassin — highest ATK on the roster with a wipe-risk passive that doubles damage when the party is near death, tempting players to run riskier expeditions.

unique_passive.description: ATK ×1.5 when party HP is critically low — the fury of the doomed.

nodes.label: Warden
nodes.description: Occult scout — balanced stats with highest LCK for better loot, rewarding patient play with superior spoils but never leading the damage charts.

unique_passive.description: LCK ×2.0 on DOMINANT expedition results — the dark favors the prepared.

nodes.label: Plague Veteran
nodes.description: Scarred survivor — highest HP pool enables aggressive expedition selection, with a passive that grants bonus HP in high-level zones where others would falter.

unique_passive.description: HP +80 when expedition level >= 8 — old scars harden against greater threats.

nodes.label: Outrunner
nodes.description: Desperate scout — highest SPD minimizes expedition duration, with a passive that accelerates further on speed clears but contributes little to DPS checks.

unique_passive.description: SPD ×1.3 when expedition completes under 60s — flight over fight.

nodes.label: Forgemaster
nodes.description: Master smith — specializes in weapons and armor crafting at the Forge; consecutive same-recipe jobs build streak bonuses that significantly reduce craft time.

unique_passive.description: Workflow speed ×1.2 when streak count >= 3 — rhythm of the hammer.

nodes.label: Shadow Alchemist
nodes.description: Essence distiller — specializes in shadow extraction and cursed crafting at the Shadow Vault; consecutive extractions build streak bonuses that increase crit chance and purity.

unique_passive.description: Crit chance +0.15 when streak count >= 4 — refined extraction.

nodes.label: Contract Broker
nodes.description: Gold negotiator — manages contracts at the Guildhall; continuous contract processing builds momentum that compounds gold income from all sources.

unique_passive.description: Gold income ×1.15 when momentum >= 50 — deals compound over time.

archetype_balance.notes: LCK-primary Warden fills controller/support hybrid role. No pure LCK class — Warden's balanced stats make her a utility pick.

primary_stat_distribution.justification: Tank archetype — frontline protector with highest DEF/HP
Damage archetype — burst assassin with highest ATK
Support/loot archetype — balanced stats with LCK for better drops
Bruiser archetype — survivor with highest HP pool
Speed archetype — scout with highest SPD for fast clears
Production artisan — weapons and armor crafting
Production artisan — essence extraction and cursed crafting
Production artisan — contract negotiation and gold income

party_recommendations.name: Balanced Party
party_recommendations.strengths: Well-rounded, low WIPE risk, decent loot
party_recommendations.weaknesses: Slow clear times, no speed specialist

party_recommendations.name: Speed Farm Party
party_recommendations.strengths: Fastest expedition clears, high burst damage
party_recommendations.weaknesses: Squishy, high WIPE risk without tank

party_recommendations.name: Loot Party
party_recommendations.strengths: Maximum loot rolls with Warden's DOMINANT bonus

party_recommendations.name: Boss Slayer Party
party_recommendations.strengths: High survival, sustained DPS for boss phases

flags.tension: Shadow Hunters described as 'fast damage dealers' but no pure SPD combat class in source
flags.conflict: Engine needs distinct ATK and SPD archetypes for party composition diversity
options: A) Split into two classes: Shadow Hunter (ATK) and Outrunner (SPD) — both thematically derived from 'fast damage' concept
B) Make Shadow Hunter SPD-primary with high ATK base stats — hybrid approach
C) Keep Shadow Hunter ATK-primary and add separate scout class not derived from source
flags.default_applied: Option A — Split into two classes. Shadow Hunter is ATK-primary (burst damage), Outrunner is SPD-primary (speed clears). Both honor the 'fast' concept mechanically distinct.

flags.tension: Source describes three hero types but engine benefits from 5+ combat classes for roster depth
flags.conflict: Faithful adaptation would produce only 3 classes; game needs more for party composition choices
options: A) Add inferred classes (Plague Veteran, Outrunner) from thematic elements — 'expendable heroes' and 'corrupted zones' imply survivors and scouts
B) Stick to 3 classes and let player clone variants — simpler but less strategic depth
C) Use world-template.json expansions (if any) to justify additional classes
flags.default_applied: Option A — Plague Veteran derived from 'heroes are expendable' + 'plague' themes; Outrunner derived from 'shadow' + 'fast' concepts. Both are thematically justified inferences.

buildforge.note: BUILDFORGE must reference these artisan classes when generating building artisan_slots and workflow staffing requirements

actforge.note: ACTFORGE must calibrate expedition difficulty against these power levels. Level 1 party (4 heroes) = ~145 total stats. Level 20 party = ~1100 total stats.

itemforge.note: ITEMFORGE must generate equipment that fits these slot configurations. Outrunner uses double accessory for speed/loot focus.

upgradeforge.note: UPGRADEFORGE must size upgrade effects relative to hero stat growth. ATK upgrades should complement natural 4.5/level growth, not overshadow it.
